parameters.name: include_deleted
parameters.description:
When true, include soft-deleted manual products. Soft-deleted rows
have `deleted_at` populated; clients can render them muted.

parameters.name: barcode
parameters.description: EAN-8/12/13/14; non-digits are stripped and UPC-A is zero-padded

parameters.name: include_deleted
parameters.description:
When true, include soft-deleted manual products. Soft-deleted rows
have `deleted_at` populated; clients can render them muted.

parameters.name: expiring_before
parameters.description:
ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD). When set, only batches expiring strictly
before this date are returned. Undated batches are excluded.

parameters.name: include_depleted
parameters.description:
When true, also include batches that have been fully consumed
(`depleted_at IS NOT NULL`).

message.description: Human-readable description. Not localised; clients should prefer `code`.

unrestorable_ids.description:
Present on `code = batch_not_restorable` when the failure came from a
bulk call — lists the specific batches the caller needs to look at.
Absent on single-batch restore (the path already names the batch).

source.description: `cache` when served from our DB, `openfoodfacts` when fetched just now.

consume_request_id.description: Correlates the `consume` events this call wrote to the ledger.

package_quantity.description: Package size snapshot for the consumed batch, when known.

quantity.description: Amount taken from this batch, in the batch's own unit.

quantity_in_requested_unit.description:
Same amount, converted to the unit the caller requested in the
`ConsumeRequest`. Lets the client display "200 ml consumed" even
when the underlying batch stores litres.

package_quantity.description: Amount in `package_unit` for one retail package.

package_unit.description: Unit for `package_quantity`; belongs to the same family as the product.

preferred_unit.description:
Optional display unit override. Must belong to `family`. Defaults to
the family's base unit (`g` / `ml` / `piece`) when omitted.

quantity_includes_storage_vessel.description:
When true, `quantity` is the gross measured mass including the selected
container. The API stores net stock after subtracting the reusable tare
profile referenced by `storage_vessel_id`.

op.description:
JSON Patch operation. Quartermaster supports `replace` and `remove`
for product/stock update endpoints.

path.description: JSON Pointer path to the top-level resource field, e.g. `/brand`.

include_quantity.description:
Include the batch quantity/unit on the printed label. Defaults to false
because labels often stay with mutable containers after first use.

label_size.description:
Label length to print: `standard` or `small`. `small` is only supported
on continuous media and keeps a compact QR code for narrow bottles or jars.

deleted_at.description:
RFC-3339 timestamp when the product was soft-deleted. Present only
when the caller explicitly asked for deleted rows (e.g. via
`/api/v1/products/search?include_deleted=true` or the history timeline).

max_open_days.description: Maximum days this product should remain open before being discarded.

package_quantity.description: Amount in `package_unit` for one retail package when known from the catalogue.

package_unit.description: Unit for `package_quantity`; belongs to the same family as the product.

device_label.description: Optional label applied to the refresh token (shown on `/api/v1/auth/me`).

invite_code.description:
Required unless the server is in `first_run_only` mode and no users
exist yet, or in `open` mode.

payload.description: Base64-encoded printer-ready command stream for the selected driver.

ids.description:
Batch IDs to restore. All restored atomically: if any isn't
recoverable (wasn't discarded, or already restored), the whole
request rolls back with `batch_not_restorable`.

storage_vessel_id.description:
Reusable tare profile used for gross-weight entry, not a unique
physical container instance.

operation_id.description:
Existing split/repack operation to append to. When omitted, the server
creates a new operation id.

package_quantity.description: Package size snapshot for this batch, captured when the batch was added.

package_unit.description: Unit for `package_quantity`; belongs to the same family as `unit`.

batch_expires_on.description:
The batch's current expiry date (YYYY-MM-DD), if any. Lets clients
render "expiring tomorrow" context on history rows.

consume_request_id.description: Shared by all rows written by a single `POST /api/v1/stock/consume` call.

operation_id.description: Shared by all rows written by a single split/repack operation.

package_quantity.description: Package size snapshot for the event/batch, when known.

recipe_execution_id.description: Shared by consume/add rows written by one recipe execution.

next_before.description:
Pagination cursor — the last returned item's `created_at` when a
full page came back, otherwise `None`. Always pair with
`next_before_id` to fetch the next page; the pair avoids the
same-millisecond tiebreak problem that a timestamp alone can miss.

next_before_id.description:
Pagination cursor — the last returned item's `id` when a full
page came back, otherwise `None`.

to_base_milli.description:
Conversion factor to the family's base unit (`g` for mass, `ml` for
volume, `piece` for count), expressed as thousandths so we can keep it
an integer. Divide by 1000 to get the multiplier.
